_comment: Scope for 30__analysis/39__external_benchmark_check.py — cross-checks our published valuation metrics against yfinance for a sample of tickers, to catch calculation bugs (e.g. the MLI split-lag bug, #235) rather than relying on someone noticing by chance. Edit this file (no code change) to grow/shrink coverage or add/remove metrics.
_comment_always_check: Tickers always included every run regardless of the rotating sample — known regressions + high-scrutiny names.
_comment_metrics: comparison_class distinguishes VALID methodological divergence from a genuine error — see the discussion on issues #236/#241. 'should_match_live': our metric is deliberately priced off TODAY's close (23__intrinsic_value.py's TTM live path), same as the yfinance field, AND the underlying $ inputs are directly comparable — divergence beyond tolerance is a real bug signal (this is what caught #235's MLI split-lag bug). 'methodology_different': logged for trend visibility only, NEVER flagged as a bug no matter how far it diverges from a live source. Two distinct reasons land a metric here — (a) deliberately priced at the FY period_end close (market_cap_asof's fiscal-close design, documented in web/templates/help.html), or (b) priced off today's close like should_match_live, but the underlying $ basis genuinely differs from yfinance's field (see #241: our Total Stockholders Equity vs yfinance bookValue differed by a real, non-clean ~29% on a sampled ticker with no split history, and our EBITDA proxy Operating Income + D&A is sensitive to one-time charges baked into GAAP operating income in a way yfinance's own ebitda figure isn't — confirmed via a ticker with two quarters of large real impairment-driven operating losses).
